title: NOC 73102 – Plasterers, drywall installers and finishers and lathers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73102 professionals are the skilled technicians responsible for the structural and aesthetic integrity of interior and exterior wall and ceiling systems. As a TEER 2 occupation, this group includes Plasterers who produce plain or decorative surfaces, Drywall Installers who erect partition systems, and Lathers who install the supporting frameworks. In 2026, as Canada aggressively pursues "Passive House" energy-efficiency standards and large-scale hospital and transit expansions, these workers are classified as High-Impact Infrastructure Talent.

classification.teer: 2026 market aligns with high-skill apprenticeship)Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group731 – Masonry and plastering tradesMinor Group7310 – Masonry and plastering tradesNOC Code73102

duties: Blueprint & Layout: Read and interpret blueprints to determine wall and ceiling layouts and calculate material requirements.
Framework Installation: (Lathers) Layout and install metal stud framing, furring, and laths to support interior/exterior walls and partitions.
Drywall Fitting: Measure, cut, and fit drywall sheets for installation on walls, ceilings, and building partitions using hand and power tools.
Securing Materials: Position and secure drywall sheets to metal or wooden studs/joists using specialized screws, nails, or adhesives.
Corner Protection: Cut and install metal or plastic corner beads to protect exterior corners and ensure perfectly straight edges.
Joint Filling: Fill joints, nail indentations, holes, and cracks with joint compound using trowels and broad knives.
Taping & Embedding: Apply tape over joints using a taping machine or manually, embedding it into the compound for structural reinforcement.
Smooth Finishing: Apply successive coats of compound, allowing for drying time, and sand seams to a paint-ready, level-5 finish.
Ceiling Systems: Fabricate and install suspended metal ceiling grids and place acoustical or coffered panels into the grid.
Plastering & Stucco: Mix plaster ingredients; apply, level, and smooth coats of plaster or stucco over building exteriors for weatherproof surfaces.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three-to-four-year apprenticeship program is the standard route to qualification.
Experience: Alternatively, over three years of work experience in the trade and some industry-certified courses may be accepted for "Trade Qualifier" status.
Trade Certification:Compulsory in Quebec.Available but voluntary in ON, AB, BC, MB, NB, NS, PEI, and NL.
Available but voluntary in ON, AB, BC, MB, NB, NS, PEI, and NL.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for all immigrants to ensure national recognition and maximum points for PR through the Federal Skilled Trades Program.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Drywall Finishers on commercial high-rise projects or unionized infrastructure sites often earn annual salaries between $78,000 and $102,000 inclusive of overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate a steady demand for new installers as Canada accelerates the construction of high-density housing and modular building units. While some basic installation tasks are being streamlined by automation, the high-skill requirements for finishers and plasterers—particularly for decorative and high-performance surfaces—ensure robust job security for the next decade.
labour_market_demand: The demand for drywall and plastering professionals in 2026 is Good to Very Good. In Ontario and Prince Edward Island, the outlook is particularly strong due to sustained residential housing starts and significant public infrastructure investments. While the market in Quebec is currently balanced, the national trend shows a persistent shortage of "Interior Systems Mechanics" who can install complex acoustic and fire-rated assemblies. According to 2026 Job Bank projections, nearly 85% of workers in this NOC receive at least one type of non-wage benefit, reflecting high employer competition for talent.
Indoor Work Stability: Unlike many exterior construction trades, the majority of drywall and finishing work is performed indoors, providing protection from harsh Canadian winters.
Specialized High-Tech Assemblies: Gain experience with cutting-edge prefabricated wall panels, advanced fireproofing sprays, and sustainable acoustic materials.
Strong Union Environment: Benefit from high unionization rates (e.g., IUPAT) which provide premium wages, dental/medical insurance, and employer-sponsored pension plans.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC is a priority in the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws, often resulting in a faster path to Permanent Residency with lower CRS requirements.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): This NOC remains a high priority under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable route for tradespeople with at least 2 years of work experience and a job offer or certificate of qualification.
OINP Skilled Trades Stream: Highly targeted for those already working in Ontario to bridge to Permanent Residency.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): Ideal for drywallers and finishers with a job offer from an approved provincial employer.
